commands.author: steipete

target.title: fix(telegram): restore readable default text sends
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93263 by restoring readable Telegram text delivery as the default while keeping Bot API 10.1 rich messages available as an explicit account-level opt-in.

- default sends, edits, native-command replies, and draft previews use standard `sendMessage` / `editMessageText`
- `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` enables native rich messages, including block tables
- account overrides and configured default-account selection are respected
- the agent prompt advertises rich text only when the selected account enables it
- rich chunks enforce Telegram's documented 32,768-character, 500-block, 50-media, 16-level nesting, and 20-column table limits
- reply quotes, threads, buttons, link-preview behavior, media fallbacks, and outbound table-mode overrides remain intact
- empty or structurally invalid rich rendering falls back to readable source text

## Telegram specification

- Bot API 10.1 changelog: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api#june-11-2026
- Rich messages: https://core.telegram.org/bots/features#rich-messages
- Bot API methods and limits: https://core.telegram.org/bots/api

Telegram currently accepts `sendRichMessage`, but current Desktop, Web, Android, and third-party clients can render accepted messages as unsupported. The default therefore remains the broadly compatible standard text path; operators can enable native rich tables on compatible clients with:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    telegram: {
      richMessages: true,
    },
  },
}
```

## Linked context

Closes #93263.

Related rich-message work: #92679, #93286, #92258.

Supersedes the group-only rollback in #93345 by fixing the default for DMs, groups, commands, edits, and previews while preserving an explicit rich-message mode.

## Verification

Passed on exact local head:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-outbound.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.helpers.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel-actions.contract.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/channel.gateway.test.ts`
  - 12 files, 533 tests passed
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- fresh `autoreview --mode local`
  - no accepted/actionable findings

Isolated Linux proof:

- Crabbox `brisk-crab-c0e5`: `pnpm tsgo:extensions` passed; the initial test-type graph found two mock tuple inference errors
- Crabbox `golden-crab-c5fe`: `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` passed after the focused test-only fix

Azure Testbox was attempted twice but did not provision a lease before the 10-minute coordinator timeout.

## Real behavior proof

![Readable Telegram DM proof](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fd676564-d727-4f13-a264-13bcfc5d8ec6)

Behavior addressed: Telegram replies accepted by the Bot API rendered as unsupported across current clients; standard text is readable by default, while native rich messages are explicit opt-in.

Real environment tested: Contributor's local OpenClaw gateway on macOS with a real Telegram bot and direct-message chat; isolated Linux typecheck validation for the final maintainer patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Restarted the gateway, sent `Hello openclaw` and `你好` in a real Telegram DM, then ran the exact local and isolated validation commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: The attached Telegram screenshot shows readable replies for both messages. Exact-head tests cover standard and rich send/edit/draft paths, native tables, list/table chunking, structural limits, account capability selection, quote fallback, and table-mode propagation.

Observed result after fix: Standard Telegram replies render as readable messages; the final extension production and test type graphs pass.

What was not tested: Exact-head live client proof for `richMessages: true` is requested separately because current Telegram clients may still show Bot API 10.1 rich messages as unsupported.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: Azure Testbox capacity was unavailable; isolated Linux validation used Crabbox local-container instead.

## Risk

Highest risk is the split standard/rich delivery boundary. The default follows the previously shipped compatible text path, rich capability is account-scoped and opt-in, and all send/edit/draft/native-command surfaces share the selected account setting.
